title: Optimize compile time when many classes
body:
Compiling UVM without even using it takes very long. (UVM had many things commented out - it's not ready for use - see #1538).
 
This affects usability experience of UVM but doesn't directly block #1538.

On my system:

```
    1146user 452system 2:51elapsed  (13 minutes if was running on a Github Actions 2 core!)

     237 classes before specialization
     592 classes after specialization
    3919 .cpp files
    1184 .cpp _Slow files
    1760 .cpp files if combine DepSet
     592 .cpp files if no DepSet at all
```

Compiling all files as a single .cpp:

```
    all w/-O0 0m53s  (gcc only no linking)
    all w/-Os 2m44s  (gcc only no linking)
```

So we should be able to get under 1 minute wall time assuming 4+ CPUs.

Actions/ideas:

- [ ] Improve V3Dead. I think most of the generated classes really aren't used, and could be not be generated. (#2227)
- [ ] When a class is specalized through parameters, merge all of the specializations into a single .cpp file (which might then get split if too large).  This will also likely help runtime performance as there's likely a lot of shared code and opportunity for inlining.
- [ ] Avoid making multiple __DepSet's that are small (e.g. one class has two each under 2KB)
- [ ] Combine small .cpp files (measured similar to --output-split) into larger ones.  Would need to be careful that a small change doesn't require a lot of recompiles.  I likely won't get to this.
- [ ] Through some fancy algorithm, disassociate .cpp files from modules, choosing the number of .cpp files by some algorithm based on a CPU count and output-split.  I likely won't get to this.
